description_long: This is a traditional Japanese-style bungalow with a prominent, dark gray hip roof made of ceramic tiles. The exterior walls are a combination of wooden panels and shoji-style sliding doors, featuring translucent paper sections and wooden latticework. Two large lanterns hang symmetrically at the front corners, adding a decorative and functional element. The structure is single-storied, with a low, wide profile and a rectangular footprint. The windows are arranged in a horizontal band, with some sections featuring wooden bars and others covered by shoji screens. The overall appearance is minimalist yet elegant, reflecting classic Japanese residential architecture.
description: A traditional Japanese bungalow with a tiled hip roof, wooden paneling, shoji doors, and hanging lanterns. The design is minimalist and features horizontal window arrangements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, shoji doors, and two hanging lanterns at the corners.
description_view_1: Side view with a clear view of the hip roof and a band of windows with wooden bars, lanterns visible at the corners.
description_view_2: Rear view with shoji panels and wooden siding, lanterns at the corners, no visible entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a band of windows and wooden bars, lanterns at the corners.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with horizontal bands of windows and shoji panels
